Verdicts
========

Gate-checkable judgments (:need:`ADR_0015`): one ``verdict`` directive per
judged need, derived ID, status-less, staleness computed from a content
fingerprint by ``pds verdict-check``. Excluded from the status-overview
needtables by design.

.. verdict:: Triage verdict for ISSUE_0014
   :id: VERDICT_ISSUE_0014
   :links: ISSUE_0014
   :rubric: triage
   :fingerprint: sha256:d73d5279a7e8ed5a

   *This was generated by AI during triage.*

   Judged in the grilling session of 2026-06-07. **category**: feature,
   clear-cut. **state**: ready-for-agent — five design decisions resolved
   and recorded in the agent brief; normative shape pinned by
   :need:`ADR_0015` / :need:`ADR_0016`. **actionability**: acceptance
   criteria are independently verifiable; landing spots groomed
   (shared corpus-check plumbing, findings envelope).
   **duplicate-check**: ``pds search`` evidence ranked ADR_0015 (0.92)
   and no issue twin; the skill-wiring half was split to ISSUE_0022
   past the dedup gate.

.. verdict:: Triage verdict for ISSUE_0022
   :id: VERDICT_ISSUE_0022
   :links: ISSUE_0022
   :rubric: triage
   :fingerprint: sha256:ce93a76416230227

   *This was generated by AI during triage.*

   Judged in the grilling session of 2026-06-07 — and authored by hand
   via the stale-flip dance, since the ``pds fingerprint`` verb this
   issue delivers does not exist yet (the self-referential dogfood).
   **category**: improvement, clear-cut — a read-only verb plus skill
   prose. **state**: ready-for-agent — all five design decisions
   resolved (fingerprint verb folded in, /review split to
   :need:`ISSUE_0023`, pickup contract prose-only, authoring mechanics,
   fail-closed axes); an agent finishes it unattended. **actionability**:
   the brief carries the verb's JSON shape, the four axis definitions,
   the fail-closed rule, the fingerprint-after-transition ordering, and a
   dogfood — executable without this conversation. **duplicate-check**:
   ``pds dedup`` fired ``duplicate`` on :need:`ISSUE_0014` (0.58) and
   :need:`ISSUE_0023` is the deliberate /review split; no true twin.
